name: Agent Physics
core_question: What are the fundamental laws governing agent behavior in distributed systems?
description: How agents behave under load, at scale, near failure boundaries. Emergent phenomena in multi-agent systems. The 'physics' of computational organisms.
human_world_bridge: Emergent behavior in agent meshes mirrors emergent behavior in physical systems — phase transitions, critical mass, cascading failures. Findings here inform our understanding of complex adaptive systems everywhere: economies, ecosystems, epidemics.
research_axes: Phase transitions: when does a federation shift from healthy to failing?
Load distribution and pressure propagation across nodes
Information entropy in federation message flows
Scaling laws: how do federation properties change with size?
